name: Source Fidelity review for Dna Repair Enhancement bundle
summary: The staged source, study, and finding records match the cited source boundaries: the nicotinamide trials are represented as one positive high-risk immunocompetent skin-cancer signal and one null transplant-recipient boundary condition; topical photolyase and DNA repair enzyme studies are kept as small human skin biomarker or preliminary actinic-keratosis signals; NR and NMN trials are correctly treated as NAD-metabolite context without direct DNA repair endpoints.

findings.claim_or_issue: Most positive source-backed claims in the bundle are specific to UV-exposed skin, actinic keratoses, or skin-cancer prevention.
findings.why_it_matters: That boundary prevents the source set from being read as direct evidence of systemic aging-related DNA repair enhancement across tissues.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the staged outlook caveats and support-map limitations that describe the evidence as skin/UV-specific and biomarker-weighted.

findings.claim_or_issue: The NR and NMN human trials support NAD-metabolite movement and tolerability, but not direct DNA repair endpoint movement.
findings.why_it_matters: NAD-linked DNA repair is plausible taxonomy context, but treating NAD elevation itself as DNA repair efficacy would overstate the sources.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the staged finding direction as inconclusive for DNA repair and keep the outlook support role as limiting/contextual rather than supporting an upgrade.
